what: Add Lagged Columns via Join — add_lagged_columns
title: Add Lagged Columns via Join — add_lagged_columns
text: Appends lagged versions specified columns data frame using join-based approach.

title: Add Lagged Columns via Join — add_lagged_columns
code: add_lagged_columns(   data,   cols,   lag,   max_lag = lag,   by = NULL,   drop_na = FALSE,   ff_adjustment = FALSE,   data_options = NULL )

title: Add Lagged Columns via Join — add_lagged_columns
text: data data frame containing variables lag. cols character vector specifying names columns lagged. column produces new column suffixed _lag. lag integer lubridate::periods() object, e.g., months(1), specifying minimum lag (inclusive) apply. max_lag integer lubridate::periods() object specifying maximum lag (inclusive) apply. Defaults lag (exact lag). optional character vector specifying grouping columns (e.g., stock identifier). Lagged values matched within groups. Defaults NULL. drop_na logical value. TRUE, NA values source columns excluded matching, lookup skips missing observations. Applied independently per column. Defaults FALSE. ff_adjustment logical value. TRUE, last observation per year (within group defined ) retained source lagged values, following Fama-French conventions annual accounting data. Defaults FALSE. data_options list class tidyfinance_data_options (created via data_options()) specifying column name mappings. date element used specify date column. Uses data_options() default NULL: "date" = "date".

title: Add Lagged Columns via Join — add_lagged_columns
text: data frame rows data new columns appended, suffixed _lag. Unmatched rows receive NA lagged columns.

title: Add Lagged Columns via Join — add_lagged_columns
text: lag == max_lag (default), equi-join used: source dates shifted forward lag matched exactly. lag < max_lag, inequality join used: row, recent source value within window [date - max_lag, date - lag] selected. combination date columns must unique data. NULL, dates alone must unique.

title: Create Breakpoint Options for Portfolio Sorting — breakpoint_options
text: n_portfolios Integer, optional. number portfolios create. Must positive integer. provided, defaults NULL. percentiles Numeric vector, optional. vector percentile thresholds defining breakpoints. value must 0 1. provided, defaults NULL. breakpoints_exchanges Character vector, optional. non-empty vector specifying exchange compute breakpoints. provided, defaults NULL. smooth_bunching Logical, optional. Indicates whether smooth bunching applied. Defaults FALSE. breakpoints_min_size_threshold Numeric, optional. set value 0 1, stocks market capitalization quantile excluded breakpoint computation. quantile computed among breakpoints_exchanges stocks specified, otherwise among stocks. Requires market capitalization column data (see data_options()). Defaults NULL (size filtering). ... Additional optional arguments. captured resulting structure list.

what: Compute Breakpoints Based on Sorting Variable — compute_breakpoints
title: Compute Breakpoints Based on Sorting Variable — compute_breakpoints
text: Computes breakpoints based specified sorting. can optionally filter data exchanges lagged size quantiles computing breakpoints. function requires either number portfolios created specific percentiles breakpoints, . function also optionally handles cases sorting variable clusters edges, assigning extreme values edges attempting compute equally populated breakpoints remaining values.

title: Compute Breakpoints Based on Sorting Variable — compute_breakpoints
code: compute_breakpoints(   data,   sorting_variable,   breakpoint_options,   data_options = NULL )

title: Compute Breakpoints Based on Sorting Variable — compute_breakpoints
text: data data frame containing dataset breakpoint computation. sorting_variable character string specifying column name data used determining breakpoints. breakpoint_options named list breakpoint_options() breakpoints. arguments include n_portfolios optional integer specifying number equally sized portfolios create. parameter mutually exclusive percentiles. percentiles optional numeric vector specifying percentiles determining breakpoints portfolios. parameter mutually exclusive n_portfolios. breakpoints_exchanges optional character vector specifying exchange names filter data computing breakpoints. Exchanges must stored column given data_options (defaults exchange). NULL, filtering applied. smooth_bunching optional logical parameter specifying attempt smoothing non-extreme portfolios sorting variable bunches extremes (TRUE), (FALSE, default). cases, smoothing result equal-sized portfolios edges due multiple clusters. sufficiently large bunching detected, percentiles ignored equally-spaced portfolios returned cases warning. breakpoints_min_size_threshold optional numeric value 0 1 (exclusive). set, stocks market capitalization quantile excluded breakpoint computation. quantile computed among breakpoints_exchanges stocks specified, otherwise among stocks. Requires market capitalization column data (column name determined data_options). data_options list class tidyfinance_data_options (created via data_options()) specifying column name mappings. exchange element used specify exchange column, mktcap_lag used specify market capitalization. Uses data_options() default NULL: "exchange" = "exchange" "mktcap_lag" = "mktcap_lag".

text: numeric vector breakpoints desired length.

title: Compute Long-Short Returns — compute_long_short_returns
text: Calculates long-short returns based returns portfolios. long-short return computed difference returns "top" "bottom" portfolios. direction calculation can adjusted based whether return "bottom" portfolio subtracted added return "top" portfolio.

title: Compute Long-Short Returns — compute_long_short_returns
code: compute_long_short_returns(   data,   direction = "top_minus_bottom",   data_options = NULL )

title: Compute Long-Short Returns — compute_long_short_returns
text: data data frame containing portfolio returns. data frame must include columns portfolio identifier, date, return measurements (specified data_options). direction character string specifying direction long-short return calculation. can either "top_minus_bottom" "bottom_minus_top". Default "top_minus_bottom". set "bottom_minus_top", return computed (bottom - top). data_options list class tidyfinance_data_options (created via data_options()) specifying column name mappings. date element used specify date column, ret_excess element used specify excess return column, portfolio used specify assigned portfolio. Uses data_options() default NULL: "date" = "date", "ret_excess" = "ret_excess", "portfolio" = "portfolio".

title: Compute Long-Short Returns — compute_long_short_returns
text: data frame columns date, return measurement types ("ret_measure" column), computed long-short returns. data frame arranged date pivoted return measurement types columns corresponding long-short returns.

title: Compute Long-Short Returns — compute_long_short_returns
code: set.seed(42) data <- data.frame(   permno = 1:100,   date = rep(     seq.Date(from = as.Date("2020-01-01"), by = "month", length.out = 100),     each = 10   ),   mktcap_lag = runif(100, 100, 1000),   ret_excess = rnorm(100),   size = runif(100, 50, 150) )  portfolio_returns <- compute_portfolio_returns(   data, "size", "univariate",   breakpoint_options_main = breakpoint_options(n_portfolios = 5) )  compute_long_short_returns(portfolio_returns) #> # A tibble: 100 × 4 #>    date       ret_excess_vw ret_excess_ew ret_excess_vw_capped #>    <date>             <dbl>         <dbl>                <dbl> #>  1 2020-01-01     -0.833          -0.650               -0.833  #>  2 2020-02-01      0.0996         -0.0632               0.0996 #>  3 2020-03-01      0.658           0.687                0.673  #>  4 2020-04-01      0.998           1.05                 1.01   #>  5 2020-05-01      0.567           0.747                0.567  #>  6 2020-06-01     -1.13           -0.817               -1.05   #>  7 2020-07-01      1.66            1.27                 1.66   #>  8 2020-08-01      0.922           1.23                 0.948  #>  9 2020-09-01     -0.488           0.137               -0.488  #> 10 2020-10-01     -0.000934        0.0858               0.0389 #> # ℹ 90 more rows

title: Compute Portfolio Returns — compute_portfolio_returns
text: Computes individual portfolio returns based specified sorting variables sorting methods. portfolios can rebalanced every period annual frequency specifying rebalancing month, applicable monthly return frequency. function supports univariate bivariate sorts, latter supporting dependent independent sorting methods.

title: Compute Portfolio Returns — compute_portfolio_returns
code: compute_portfolio_returns(   data,   sorting_variables,   sorting_method,   rebalancing_month = NULL,   breakpoint_options_main,   breakpoint_options_secondary = NULL,   breakpoint_function_main = compute_breakpoints,   breakpoint_function_secondary = compute_breakpoints,   min_portfolio_size = 1L,   cap_weight = 0.8,   data_options = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Compute Portfolio Returns — compute_portfolio_returns
text: data data frame containing dataset portfolio assignment return computation. panel data must include individual stock identifiers time point. must contain columns sorting variables excess returns. Additionally, lagged market capitalization required value-weighted returns (see parameter data_options). sorting_variables character vector specifying column names data used sorting determining portfolio assignments. univariate sorts, provide single variable. bivariate sorts, provide two variables, first string refers main variable second string refers secondary ("control") variable. sorting_method string specifying sorting method used. Possible values : "univariate": single sorting variable. "bivariate-dependent": two sorting variables, main sort depends secondary variable. "bivariate-independent": two independent sorting variables. bivariate sorts, portfolio returns averaged controlling sorting variable (.e., second sorting variable), portfolio returns main sorting variable (given first element sorting_variables) returned. rebalancing_month integer 1 12 specifying month form portfolios held constant one year. example, setting 7 creates portfolios July held constant June following year. default NULL corresponds periodic rebalancing. breakpoint_options_main named list breakpoint_options() passed breakpoint_function_main main sorting variable. breakpoint_options_secondary optional named list breakpoint_options() passed breakpoint_function_secondary. breakpoint_function_main function compute breakpoints main sorting variable. default set compute_breakpoints(). breakpoint_function_secondary function compute breakpoints secondary sorting variable. default set compute_breakpoints(). min_portfolio_size integer specifying minimum number firms required reported portfolio cross-section given date (default 1L, .e. least one observation per reported portfolio). univariate bivariate sorts threshold applied firm count per (portfolio, date) reported cross-section: univariate sorts firms per portfolio-date; bivariate sorts firms per main-portfolio-date summed across secondary buckets. Cross-sections threshold returns set NA. typical value 5L (Fama-French convention). Set 0L deactivate check entirely. cap_weight numeric value 0 1 specifying percentile market capitalization capped per date computing capped value-weighted excess returns (.e., ret_excess_vw_capped). Defaults 0.8. data_options list class tidyfinance_data_options (created via data_options()) specifying column name mappings. id element used specify entity (.e., firm), date used specify date column, ret_excess used specify excess return column, mktcap_lag used specify market capitalization. Uses data_options() default NULL: "id" = "permno", "date" = "date", "ret_excess" = "ret_excess", "mktcap_lag" = "mktcap_lag". quiet logical value indicating whether suppress informational messages missing values output panel (default FALSE).

title: Compute Portfolio Returns — compute_portfolio_returns
text: data frame computed portfolio returns complete panel (portfolio-date combinations), containing following columns: portfolio: portfolio identifier. date: date portfolio return. ret_excess_vw: value-weighted excess return portfolio (computed data contains lagged market capitalization column specified data_options(), defaults mktcap_lag). NA insufficient observations portfolio-date. ret_excess_ew: equal-weighted excess return portfolio. NA insufficient observations portfolio-date. ret_excess_vw_capped: capped value-weighted excess return portfolio (computed data contains lagged market capitalization column specified data_options(), defaults mktcap_lag). Weights computed using market capitalization capped cap_weight percentile per date. NA insufficient observations portfolio-date.

title: Compute Portfolio Returns — compute_portfolio_returns
text: function checks consistency provided arguments. univariate sorts, single sorting variable corresponding number portfolios must provided. bivariate sorts, two sorting variables two corresponding numbers portfolios (percentiles) required. sorting method determines portfolios assigned returns computed. function handles missing extreme values appropriately based specified sorting method rebalancing frequency.

title: Compute Portfolio Returns — compute_portfolio_returns
text: Ensure data contains required columns: specified sorting variables excess returns (see options defaults set data_options). function stop throw error required columns missing.

what: Compute a Rolling Value by Period — compute_rolling_value
title: Compute a Rolling Value by Period — compute_rolling_value
text: Applies arbitrary summary function rolling time-period windows. window spans periods units period (e.g., 12 months). calling .f, rows missing values dropped window; fewer min_obs rows remain, result NA_real_ instead.

title: Compute a Rolling Value by Period — compute_rolling_value
code: compute_rolling_value(   data,   .f,   period = "month",   periods = 12,   min_obs = periods,   data_options = NULL )

title: Compute a Rolling Value by Period — compute_rolling_value
text: data data frame date column class Date, named according data_options$date (default "date"). .f function applied window. Receives data-frame slice (complete cases ) must return single scalar value. period string specifying period rolling windows (e.g., "month", "quarter", "year"). periods Number periods include rolling window. min_obs Minimum number non-missing rows required per window. Defaults periods. data_options list class tidyfinance_data_options (created via data_options()) specifying column name mappings. date element used specify date column. Uses data_options() default NULL: "date" = "date".

title: Compute a Rolling Value by Period — compute_rolling_value
text: numeric vector aligned rows data.

title: Compute a Rolling Value by Period — compute_rolling_value
code: library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  # Rolling standard deviation set.seed(42) df <- tibble(   date = seq.Date(     from = as.Date("2020-01-01"),     by = "month",     length.out = 24   ),   value = rnorm(24) )  df |>   mutate(     rolling_sd = compute_rolling_value(       pick(everything()),       .f = ~ sd(.x$value, na.rm = TRUE),       period = "month",       periods = 4,       min_obs = 2     )   ) #> # what: Create Summary Statistics for Specified Variables — create_summary_statistics
title: Create Summary Statistics for Specified Variables — create_summary_statistics
text: Computes set summary statistics numeric integer variables data frame. allows users select specific variables summarization can calculate statistics whole dataset within groups specified argument. Additional detail levels quantiles can included.

title: Create Summary Statistics for Specified Variables — create_summary_statistics
code: create_summary_statistics(   data,   ...,   by = NULL,   detail = FALSE,   drop_na = FALSE )

title: Create Summary Statistics for Specified Variables — create_summary_statistics
text: data data frame containing variables summarized. ... Comma-separated list unquoted variable names data frame summarize. variables must either numeric, integer, logical. optional unquoted variable name group data summarizing. NULL (default), summary statistics computed across observations. detail logical flag indicating whether compute detailed summary statistics, including additional quantiles. Defaults FALSE, computes basic statistics (n, mean, sd, min, median, max). TRUE, additional quantiles (1%, 5%, 10%, 25%, 75%, 90%, 95%, 99%) computed. drop_na logical flag indicating whether drop missing values variable (default FALSE).

title: Create Summary Statistics for Specified Variables — create_summary_statistics
text: tibble summary statistics selected variable. specified, output includes grouping variable well. row represents variable (group used), column contains computed statistics.

title: Create Summary Statistics for Specified Variables — create_summary_statistics
text: function first checks specified variables type numeric, integer, logical. variables meet criterion, function stops returns error message indicating non-conforming variables. basic set summary statistics includes count non-NA values (n), mean, standard deviation (sd), minimum (min), median (q50), maximum (max). detail TRUE, function also computes 1st, 5th, 10th, 25th, 75th, 90th, 95th, 99th percentiles. Summary statistics computed variable specified .... variable provided, statistics computed within level variable.

title: Create Summary Statistics for Specified Variables — create_summary_statistics
code: data <- data.frame(   ret = c(0.01, -0.02, 0.03, NA, 0.005),   size = c(100, 200, 150, 300, 250),   group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "A") )  # Basic summary across all observations create_summary_statistics(data, ret, size) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   variable     n  mean    sd   min   q50   max #>   <chr>    <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 ret          4    NA  NA      NA    NA    NA #> 2 size         5   200  79.1   100   200   300  # Grouped summary create_summary_statistics(data, ret, size, by = group) #> # A tibble: 4 × 8 #>   variable group     n      mean       sd    min     q50    max #>   <chr>    <chr> <int>     <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 ret      A         3  -0.00167   0.0161  -0.02   0.005   0.01 #> 2 ret      B         1  NA        NA       NA     NA      NA    #> 3 size     A         3 183.       76.4    100    200     250    #> 4 size     B         2 225       106.     150    225     300     # Detailed quantiles create_summary_statistics(data, ret, detail = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 1 × 15 #>   variable     n  mean    sd   min   q01   q05   q10   q25   q50   q75   q90 #>   <chr>    <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 ret          4    NA    NA    NA    NA    NA    NA    NA    NA    NA    NA #> # ℹ 3 more variables: q95 <dbl>, q99 <dbl>, max <dbl>

title: Create Data Options — data_options
text: id character string representing entity variable (defaults "permno"). date character string representing date variable (defaults "date"). exchange character string representing exchange variable (defaults "exchange"). mktcap_lag character string representing market capitalization lag variable (defaults "mktcap_lag"). ret_excess character string representing excess return variable (defaults "ret_excess"). portfolio character string representing portfolio variable (defaults "portfolio"). siccd character string representing Standard Industrial Classification code variable (defaults "siccd"). price character string representing (adjusted) price variable (defaults "prc_adj"). listing_age character string representing listing age variable (defaults "listing_age"). character string representing book equity variable (defaults ""). earnings character string representing earnings variable (defaults "ib", Compustat income extraordinary items column). ... what: Download and Process Data Based on Domain and Dataset — download_data
title: Download and Process Data Based on Domain and Dataset — download_data
text: Downloads processes data based specified domain (e.g., Fama-French factors, Global Q factors, macro predictors), dataset, date range. function checks specified domain supported delegates appropriate function downloading processing data.

title: Download and Process Data Based on Domain and Dataset — download_data
code: download_data(   domain = NULL,   dataset = NULL,   start_date = NULL,   end_date = NULL,   type = deprecated(),   ... )

title: Download and Process Data Based on Domain and Dataset — download_data
text: domain domain dataset download, using names returned list_supported_datasets(): "Fama-French", "Global Q", "Goyal-Welch", "WRDS", "Pseudo Data", "Index Constituents", "FRED", "Stock Prices", "Open Source Asset Pricing", "Global Factor Data", "Pastor-Stambaugh", "Stambaugh-Yuan", "Tidy Finance". Use "Pseudo Data" obtain pseudo data schema "WRDS" testing rendering without WRDS subscription. previous machine-readable names (e.g., "famafrench", "wrds", "pseudo", "tidyfinance") soft-deprecated still accepted. dataset Optional. specific dataset download within domain. start_date Optional. character string Date object "YYYY-MM-DD" format specifying start date data. provided, full dataset subset returned, depending dataset type. end_date Optional. character string Date object "YYYY-MM-DD" format specifying end date data. provided, full dataset subset returned, depending dataset type. type Use domain dataset instead. ... Additional arguments passed specific download functions depending domain. instance, domain "Index Constituents", arguments passed download_data_constituents(). domain "Global Factor Data", dataset argument arguments region, factors, classification, frequency, weighting passed download_data_jkp(). domain "Tidy Finance" dataset "factor_library", arguments either filter inputs (e.g., sorting_variable, rebalancing, fill_all) explicit ids vector bypasses grid filter downloads specified portfolios directly via download_factor_library_ids(); see download_data_huggingface() details.

title: Download Constituent Data — download_data_constituents
text: Downloads processes constituent data specified financial index. data fetched remote CSV file, filtered, cleaned provide relevant information constituents.

title: Download Constituent Data — download_data_constituents
code: download_data_constituents(index)

title: Download Constituent Data — download_data_constituents
text: index character string specifying name financial index download constituent data. index must one supported indexes listed list_supported_indexes().

title: Download Constituent Data — download_data_constituents
text: tibble five columns: symbol ticker symbol equity constituent. name name equity constituent. location location company based. exchange exchange equity traded. currency currency equity traded, derived exchange. tibble filtered exclude non-equity entries, blacklisted symbols, empty names, entries containing index name "CASH".

title: Download Constituent Data — download_data_constituents

what: Download data from a Hugging Face dataset — download_data_huggingface
title: Download data from a Hugging Face dataset — download_data_huggingface
text: Downloads data supported Hugging Face dataset. "high_frequency_sp500", parquet files filtered date range row-bound. "factor_library", portfolio characteristics selected via filter_factor_library_grid(), matching return data downloaded, result filtered start_date/end_date supplied. "factor_library_grid", grid returned via download_factor_library_grid().

title: Download data from a Hugging Face dataset — download_data_huggingface
code: download_data_huggingface(   dataset = NULL,   start_date = NULL,   end_date = NULL,   type = deprecated(),   ... )

title: Download data from a Hugging Face dataset — download_data_huggingface
text: dataset Character(1). dataset download. Supported values "high_frequency_sp500", "factor_library", "factor_library_grid". start_date Date character. Start date (inclusive) "YYYY-MM-DD" format. Used "high_frequency_sp500" "factor_library". omitted "factor_library", full return history returned; "high_frequency_sp500" falls back built-sample window. end_date Date character. End date (inclusive) "YYYY-MM-DD" format. See start_date. type Use dataset instead. ... dataset = "factor_library": either named arguments used filter portfolio grid, ids = <vector> bypass grid filter download specific portfolios directly via download_factor_library_ids(). Filter arguments take form column = value, value may vector match multiple levels. Optionally pass fill_all = TRUE leave unspecified columns unrestricted (default: FALSE, .e. unspecified columns fixed defaults listed ). Passing NULL parameter removes filter entirely, returning values column (e.g., min_size_quantile = NULL includes size groups). Passing unrecognised column name raises error listing supported names. ids combined filter arguments. Ignored dataset != "factor_library". See Details section supported columns defaults.

title: Download data from a Hugging Face dataset — download_data_huggingface
text: tibble downloaded data. "high_frequency_sp500", contains 5-second aggregated orderbook snapshots filtered requested date range. "factor_library", contains portfolio return data joined full grid metadata matched portfolio IDs.

title: Download data from a Hugging Face dataset — download_data_huggingface
text: Note dataset = "factor_library" defaults: defaults reflect one common portfolio construction choice, may suit every research question. Always verify selected combination matches intended design. Supported columns defaults ...: sorting_variable: Required. firm characteristic used sort stocks portfolios (e.g., "" market equity, "bm" book--market). default applied. min_size_quantile (defaults 0.2): Fraction smallest stocks (market cap) excluded portfolio universe. 0.2 drops bottom 20%. exclude_financials (defaults FALSE): Whether drop financial-sector stocks (SIC 6000-6999) universe. exclude_utilities (default: FALSE): Whether drop utility-sector stocks (SIC 4900-4999) universe. exclude_negative_earnings (defaults FALSE): Whether drop firms negative earnings sorting. sorting_variable_lag (defaults "6m"): Lag applied sorting variable portfolio assignment (e.g., "6m" = 6-month lag). rebalancing (defaults "monthly"): frequently portfolios reformed: "monthly" "annual". n_portfolios_main (defaults 10): Number quantile groups (e.g., 10 decile portfolios). sorting_method (defaults "univariate"): Whether portfolios formed single sort ("univariate") sequential double sort ("sequential"). n_portfolios_secondary (defaults NULL): Number groups secondary sort variable. Required sorting_method "univariate". breakpoints_exchanges (defaults : "NYSE"): Exchange(s) used compute breakpoints. "NYSE" uses NYSE-listed stocks define quantile cutoffs (conventional Fama-French approach). breakpoints_min_size_threshold (defaults NULL): Minimum market-cap threshold (USD) applied computing breakpoints. NULL means minimum-size screen applied. weighting_scheme (defaults "VW"): Return weighting within portfolios: "VW" value-weighted "EW" equal-weighted.

title: Download and Process Global Factor Data — download_data_jkp
text: dataset Global Factor Data product download, one : "factors" (characteristic-managed portfolio returns, default), "portfolios" (underlying low/middle/high portfolios make long-short factor), "industry" (industry returns), "nyse_cutoffs" (NYSE size breakpoints), "return_cutoffs" (return winsorization cutoffs). region character string region country download, using codes availability manifest (e.g., "usa", "world", "developed", "emerging", ISO-3 country code "jpn"). Defaults "usa". Ignored reference datasets "nyse_cutoffs" "return_cutoffs". Call list_supported_jkp_factors() see available regions. factors character string selecting factor content "factors" "portfolios" datasets. "factors": "mkt" (market factor), "all_factors" (153 factors), "all_themes" (13 themes), single theme (e.g., "value", "momentum"), single factor code (e.g., "be_me", "ret_12_1"). "portfolios": single factor code. Defaults "all_factors". Call list_supported_jkp_factors(region, dataset) see values available region. classification industry classification "industry" dataset, either "gics" (default) "ff49" (Fama-French 49 industries). frequency data frequency, either "monthly" (default) "daily". "industry" dataset available monthly frequency. "return_cutoffs", frequency selects monthly daily cutoff file. weighting portfolio weighting scheme: "vw_cap" (capped value-weighted, default), "vw" (value-weighted), "ew" (equal-weighted). Ignored reference datasets. start_date Optional. character string Date object "YYYY-MM-DD" format specifying start date data. provided, full dataset returned. end_date Optional. character string Date object "YYYY-MM-DD" format specifying end date data. provided, full dataset returned.

Daily data starts 1954-01-04 availability DTB3, monthly data starts 1934-01-01.

title: Download Risk-Free Rate Data — download_data_risk_free
text: tibble two columns: date date observation. risk_free risk-free rate period.

title: Download Risk-Free Rate Data — download_data_risk_free
text: series quoted annualised bank discount rates 360-day basis. Given annualised discount rate d T-bill n days maturity, holding-period return HPR = d * n/360 / (1 - d * n/360), converted target period length via (1 + HPR)^(target/source) - 1. series spliced 2001-07-01: Pre-2001: TB3MS (monthly) DTB3 (daily), 3-month T-bill n = 90. Monthly conversion uses exponent 1/3; daily conversion uses exponent 1/63 (approx. trading days per quarter). 2001: DTB4WK, 4-week T-bill n = 28. monthly data, last non-NA observation per calendar month taken exponent 365/(28*12). daily data, observations used -exponent 1/20 (approx. trading days per 4-week period). Business-day gaps daily series (e.g. holidays) handled forward-filling recent available rate. Monthly data starts 1934-01-01 (TB3MS). Daily data starts 1954-01-04 due availability DTB3.

title: Estimate Rolling Betas — estimate_betas
text: Estimates rolling betas given linear model using fast, vectorized approach. Instead fitting one regression per stock estimation window, function collapses data additive cumulants (entries moment matrices \(X'X\) \(X'y\)) per stock period, aggregates cumulants rolling calendar windows single pass slider::slide_index_sum(), recovers coefficients via closed-form OLS solution \(\hat\beta = (X'X)^{-1} X'y\). produces estimates numerically identical per-window regression, fast enough per-stock nesting parallelization required.

title: Estimate Rolling Betas — estimate_betas
code: estimate_betas(data, model, lookback, min_obs = NULL, data_options = NULL)

title: Estimate Rolling Betas — estimate_betas
text: data data frame containing data date identifier (defaults date), stock identifier (defaults permno), variables used model. model character string describing model estimated (e.g., "ret_excess ~ mkt_excess + hml + smb"). lookback Period object specifying number months, days, hours, minutes, seconds look back estimating rolling model. min_obs integer specifying minimum number observations required estimate model. Defaults 80% lookback. Windows fewer observations dropped output. data_options list class tidyfinance_data_options (created via data_options()) specifying column name mappings. id used specify entity (.e., firm), date element used specify date column. Uses data_options() default NULL: "id" = "permno" "date" = "date".

title: Estimate Rolling Betas — estimate_betas
text: data frame estimated betas entity period. contains entity date identifiers, intercept column (model includes one), one beta_<variable> column per regressor.

title: Estimate Fama-MacBeth Regressions — estimate_fama_macbeth

title: Estimate Fama-MacBeth Regressions — estimate_fama_macbeth
text: data data frame containing data regression. must include column representing time periods (defaults date) variables specified model. model character string describing model estimated cross-section (e.g., "ret_excess ~ beta + bm + log_mktcap"). vcov character string indicating type standard errors compute. Options "iid" independent identically distributed errors "newey-west" Newey-West standard errors. Default "newey-west". vcov_options list additional arguments passed NeweyWest() function vcov = "newey-west". can include options lag, specifies number lags use Newey-West covariance matrix estimation, prewhite, indicates whether apply prewhitening transformation. Default empty list. data_options list class tidyfinance_data_options (created via data_options()) specifying column name mappings. date element used specify date column. Uses data_options() default NULL: "date" = "date". detail logical value indicating whether return additional summary statistics. FALSE (default), function returns coefficient estimates. TRUE, returns list two elements: coefficients (usual estimates table) summary_statistics (one-row tibble average cross-sectional R-squared average number observations per cross-section).

title: Estimate Fama-MacBeth Regressions — estimate_fama_macbeth
text: detail = FALSE (default), tibble columns factor, risk_premium, n (number time periods), standard_error, t_statistic. detail = TRUE, named list two elements: coefficients tibble described . summary_statistics one-row tibble r_squared (mean cross-sectional R-squared) n_obs (mean cross-sectional observation count).

title: Estimate Fama-MacBeth Regressions — estimate_fama_macbeth
text: Fama, E. F., & MacBeth, J. D. (1973). Risk, return, equilibrium: Empirical tests. Journal Political Economy, 81(3), 607-636. doi:10.1086/260061 Newey, W. K., & West, K. D. (1987). simple, positive semi-definite, heteroskedasticity autocorrelation consistent covariance matrix. Econometrica, 55(3), 703-708. doi:10.2307/1913610

title: Estimate Fama-MacBeth Regressions — estimate_fama_macbeth
code: set.seed(1234)  data <- tibble::tibble(   date = rep(seq.Date(from = as.Date("2020-01-01"),                       to = as.Date("2020-12-01"), by = "month"), each = 50),   permno = rep(1:50, times = 12),   ret_excess = rnorm(600, 0, 0.1),   beta = rnorm(600, 1, 0.2),   bm = rnorm(600, 0.5, 0.1),   log_mktcap = rnorm(600, 10, 1) )  estimate_fama_macbeth(data, "ret_excess ~ beta + bm + log_mktcap") #> # title: Estimate a Linear Model — estimate_model
text: Estimates linear model specified one independent variables. checks presence specified independent variables dataset whether dataset sufficient number observations. Depending output parameter, returns model's coefficients, t-statistics, residuals, combination named list.

title: Estimate a Linear Model — estimate_model
code: estimate_model(data, model, min_obs = 1, output = "coefficients")

title: Estimate a Linear Model — estimate_model
text: data data frame containing dependent variable one independent variables. model character describes model estimated (e.g., "ret_excess ~ mkt_excess + hml + smb"). min_obs minimum number observations required estimate model. Defaults 1. output character vector specifying return. Must contain one "coefficients" (default), "residuals", "tstats". single value provided, corresponding object returned directly. multiple values provided, named list returned.

title: Estimate a Linear Model — estimate_model
text: output contains single value: data frame coefficients t-statistics, numeric vector residuals. output contains multiple values: named list requested elements. Coefficients t-statistics returned data frames column names corresponding model terms. Residuals returned numeric vector length nrow(data) NA rows missing data insufficient observations.

title: Create Filter Options — filter_options
text: Creates list filter options class tidyfinance_filter_options used sample construction TidyFinance-related functions. options control observations retained portfolio sorting.

title: Create Filter Options — filter_options
code: filter_options(   exclude_financials = FALSE,   exclude_utilities = FALSE,   min_stock_price = NULL,   min_size_quantile = NULL,   min_listing_age = NULL,   exclude_negative_book_equity = FALSE,   exclude_negative_earnings = FALSE,   ... )

title: Create Filter Options — filter_options
text: exclude_financials logical indicating whether exclude financial firms (SIC codes 6000–6799). Defaults FALSE. exclude_utilities logical indicating whether exclude utility firms (SIC codes 4900–4999). Defaults FALSE. min_stock_price single positive numeric specifying minimum stock price required include observation. NULL (default) applies price filter. min_size_quantile single numeric strictly 0 1 specifying minimum cross-sectional size quantile (based lagged market cap) required include observation. NULL (default) applies size quantile filter. cutoff computed NYSE stocks . requires exchange column data (mapped via data_options()); error raised missing. min_listing_age single non-negative integer numeric specifying minimum number months stock must listed CRSP. NULL (default) applies listing age filter. exclude_negative_book_equity logical indicating whether exclude observations non-positive book equity. Defaults FALSE. exclude_negative_earnings logical indicating whether exclude observations non-positive earnings. Defaults FALSE. ... Additional arguments included filter options list.

title: Create Filter Options — filter_options
text: list class tidyfinance_filter_options containing specified filter options.

title: Create Filter Options — filter_options

title: Filter Sorting Data — filter_sorting_data
text: Applies sample construction filters data frame portfolio sorting. Filters applied fixed order: financials exclusion, utilities exclusion, minimum stock price, minimum size quantile, minimum listing age, positive book equity, positive earnings. informational message emitted filter actually removes least one observation.

title: Filter Sorting Data — filter_sorting_data
code: filter_sorting_data(   data,   filter_options = NULL,   data_options = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Filter Sorting Data — filter_sorting_data
text: data data frame containing stock-level panel data filtered. filter_options list class tidyfinance_filter_options created filter_options(). NULL (default), defaults filter_options() used (.e., filters applied). arguments accepted filter_options() include exclude_financials logical indicating whether exclude financial firms (SIC codes 6000–6799). Defaults FALSE. exclude_utilities logical indicating whether exclude utility firms (SIC codes 4900–4999). Defaults FALSE. min_stock_price single positive numeric specifying minimum stock price required include observation. NULL (default) applies price filter. min_size_quantile single numeric strictly 0 1 specifying minimum cross-sectional size quantile (based lagged market cap) required include observation. NULL (default) applies size quantile filter. cutoff computed NYSE stocks ; exchange column (mapped via data_options()) must present data error raised. min_listing_age single non-negative integer numeric specifying minimum number months stock must listed CRSP. NULL (default) applies listing age filter. exclude_negative_book_equity logical indicating whether exclude observations non-positive book equity. Defaults FALSE. exclude_negative_earnings logical indicating whether exclude observations non-positive earnings. Defaults FALSE. data_options list class tidyfinance_data_options (created via data_options()) specifying column name mappings. siccd element used specify SIC code column, price used specify (adjusted) price column, mktcap_lag used specify market capitalization column, date used specify date column, listing_age used specify listing age column, used specify book equity column, earnings used specify earnings column. Uses data_options() default NULL: "siccd" = "siccd", "price" = "prc_adj", "exchange" = "exchange" "mktcap_lag" = "mktcap_lag", "date" = "date", "listing_age" = "listing_age", "" = "", "earnings" = "ib". quiet logical indicating whether informational messages suppressed. Defaults FALSE.

title: Filter Sorting Data — filter_sorting_data
text: filtered data frame, preserving class structure input.

title: Filter Sorting Data — filter_sorting_data
code: data <- data.frame(   permno = 1:5,   date = as.Date("2020-01-01"),   siccd = c(6100, 2000, 4950, 3000, 6500),   prc_adj = c(5, 0.5, 15, 20, 10) )  data |>   filter_sorting_data(     filter_options = filter_options(       exclude_financials = TRUE,       min_stock_price = 1     )   ) #> Filter 'exclude_financials': removed 2 observations. #> Filter 'min_stock_price': removed 1 observation. #>   permno       date siccd prc_adj #> 1      3 2020-01-01  4950      15 #> 2      4 2020-01-01  3000      20

title: Implement Portfolio Sort — implement_portfolio_sort
text: convenience wrapper combines sample construction filtering portfolio return computation single call. Equivalent calling filter_sorting_data() followed compute_portfolio_returns().

title: Implement Portfolio Sort — implement_portfolio_sort
code: implement_portfolio_sort(   data,   sorting_variables,   sorting_method,   rebalancing_month = NULL,   portfolio_sort_options,   breakpoint_function_main = compute_breakpoints,   breakpoint_function_secondary = compute_breakpoints,   min_portfolio_size = 1L,   cap_weight = 0.8,   data_options = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Implement Portfolio Sort — implement_portfolio_sort
text: data data frame containing stock-level panel data. sorting_variables character vector one two column names sort portfolios . sorting_method string specifying sorting method: "univariate", "bivariate-dependent", "bivariate-independent". rebalancing_month optional integer specifying month portfolios rebalanced annually. NULL (default) means monthly rebalancing. portfolio_sort_options list class tidyfinance_portfolio_sort_options created portfolio_sort_options(), bundling filter breakpoint specifications. arguments accepted portfolio_sort_options() include filter_options list class tidyfinance_filter_options created filter_options(), NULL (default, applies filters). Options include exclude_financials, exclude_utilities, min_stock_price, min_size_quantile, min_listing_age, exclude_negative_book_equity, exclude_negative_earnings. breakpoint_options_main list class tidyfinance_breakpoint_options created breakpoint_options(), specifying breakpoints primary sorting variable. Options include n_portfolios, percentiles, breakpoints_exchanges, smooth_bunching, breakpoints_min_size_threshold. breakpoint_options_secondary list class tidyfinance_breakpoint_options created breakpoint_options(), specifying breakpoints secondary sorting variable, NULL (default) univariate sorts. Options breakpoint_options_main. breakpoint_function_main function used compute breakpoints main sorting variable. Defaults compute_breakpoints(). breakpoint_function_secondary function used compute breakpoints secondary sorting variable. Defaults compute_breakpoints(). min_portfolio_size integer specifying minimum number firms reported portfolio cross-section given date. Defaults 1L (least one observation per reported portfolio). univariate sorts firms per portfolio-date; bivariate sorts firms per main-portfolio-date summed across secondary buckets. Cross-sections threshold returns set NA. Set 0L deactivate check. See compute_portfolio_returns(). cap_weight numeric 0 1 specifying quantile portfolio weights capped capped value-weighted return. Defaults 0.8. data_options list class tidyfinance_data_options (created via data_options()) specifying column name mappings. elements forwarded filter_sorting_data() compute_portfolio_returns(). Uses data_options() default NULL: "id" = "permno", "date" = "date", "exchange" = "exchange", "mktcap_lag" = "mktcap_lag", "ret_excess" = "ret_excess", "siccd" = "siccd", "price" = "prc_adj", "listing_age" = "listing_age", "" = "", "earnings" = "ib". quiet logical indicating whether informational messages suppressed. Defaults FALSE.

title: Implement Portfolio Sort — implement_portfolio_sort
text: data frame portfolio returns returned compute_portfolio_returns().

what: Join Lagged Variable Values over a Date Range — join_lagged_values
title: Join Lagged Variable Values over a Date Range — join_lagged_values
text: Joins lagged values selected variables one dataset (new_data) another (original_data), based date ranges defined min_lag max_lag. Unlike add_lagged_columns(), function supports joining across data frames different date grids (e.g., monthly source data quarterly target data).

title: Join Lagged Variable Values over a Date Range — join_lagged_values
code: join_lagged_values(   original_data,   new_data,   id_keys,   min_lag,   max_lag,   ff_adjustment = FALSE,   data_options = NULL )

title: Join Lagged Variable Values over a Date Range — join_lagged_values
text: original_data data frame containing target panel data. new_data data frame containing source variables lag merge. columns besides id_keys date column lagged joined. id_keys character vector specifying identifier column(s). min_lag lubridate::Period specifying lower lag bound (inclusive). max_lag lubridate::Period specifying upper lag bound (inclusive). ff_adjustment Logical; TRUE, keeps last observation per identifier year lagging (Fama-French convention). Defaults FALSE. data_options list class tidyfinance_data_options (created via data_options()) specifying column name mappings. date element used identify date column. Uses data_options() default NULL: "date" = "date".

title: Join Lagged Variable Values over a Date Range — join_lagged_values
text: data frame columns original_data plus lagged columns new_data (keeping original names).

code: portfolio_sort_options(   filter_options = NULL,   breakpoint_options_main,   breakpoint_options_secondary = NULL,   ... )

title: Create Portfolio Sort Options — portfolio_sort_options
text: filter_options list class tidyfinance_filter_options created filter_options(), NULL (default, applies filters). arguments accepted filter_options() include exclude_financials logical indicating whether exclude financial firms (SIC codes 6000–6799). Defaults FALSE. exclude_utilities logical indicating whether exclude utility firms (SIC codes 4900–4999). Defaults FALSE. min_stock_price single positive numeric specifying minimum stock price required include observation. NULL (default) applies price filter. min_size_quantile single numeric strictly 0 1 specifying minimum cross-sectional size quantile (based lagged market cap) required include observation. NULL (default) applies size quantile filter. min_listing_age single non-negative integer numeric specifying minimum number months stock must listed CRSP. NULL (default) applies listing age filter. exclude_negative_book_equity logical indicating whether exclude observations non-positive book equity. Defaults FALSE. exclude_negative_earnings logical indicating whether exclude observations non-positive earnings. Defaults FALSE. breakpoint_options_main list class tidyfinance_breakpoint_options created breakpoint_options(), specifying breakpoints primary sorting variable. arguments accepted breakpoint_options() include n_portfolios optional integer specifying number equally sized portfolios create. parameter mutually exclusive percentiles. percentiles optional numeric vector specifying percentiles determining breakpoints portfolios. parameter mutually exclusive n_portfolios. breakpoints_exchanges optional character vector specifying exchange names filter data computing breakpoints. Exchanges must stored column given data_options (defaults exchange). NULL, filtering applied. smooth_bunching optional logical parameter specifying attempt smoothing non-extreme portfolios sorting variable bunches extremes (TRUE), (FALSE, default). breakpoints_min_size_threshold optional numeric value 0 1 (exclusive). set, stocks market capitalization quantile excluded breakpoint computation. breakpoint_options_secondary list class tidyfinance_breakpoint_options created breakpoint_options(), specifying breakpoints secondary sorting variable, NULL (default) univariate sorts. arguments accepted breakpoint_options() breakpoint_options_main. ... Additional arguments included options list.

title: Create Portfolio Sort Options — portfolio_sort_options
text: list class tidyfinance_portfolio_sort_options containing specified options.

Added download_data_jkp() "Global Factor Data" domain download_data(), downloads data Global Factor Data (Jensen, Kelly, Pedersen, 2023). dataset argument selects factor returns ("factors"), underlying long-short portfolios ("portfolios"), industry returns ("industry"), reference files "nyse_cutoffs" "return_cutoffs". requested selection validated library’s live availability manifest, helper list_supported_jkp_factors() lists available regions selectors.

title: tidyfinance 0.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-25

title: tidyfinance 0.7.0
text: estimate_betas() now uses fast, vectorized closed-form approach based rolling cumulants moment matrices instead fitting one regression per stock window. removes need per-stock nesting optional furrr parallelization, use_furrr argument furrr dependency dropped. Estimates numerically identical previous regression-based implementation. Windows fewer min_obs observations now dropped output rather returned NA coefficients. package website moved package.tidy-finance.org r.tidy-finance.org. download_data() now uses human-readable domain names returned list_supported_datasets() (e.g., "Fama-French", "Global Q", "WRDS", "Tidy Finance"). "pseudo" "tidyfinance" domains renamed "Pseudo Data" "Tidy Finance". previous machine-readable domain names (e.g., "famafrench", "wrds", "pseudo", "tidyfinance") soft-deprecated still accepted. download_data_wrds_crsp() now errors informatively version = "v1" used end_date later December 2024, reflecting discontinuation CRSP legacy version end 2024. Removed “experimental” lifecycle badge assign_portfolio(), compute_breakpoints(), compute_rolling_value(), estimate_model(), join_lagged_values(), now considered stable.

title: tidyfinance 0.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-31

title: tidyfinance 0.6.0
text: Added domain = "pseudo" download_data() generating pseudo data schema corresponding real domain. Supported datasets release: "crsp_monthly", "crsp_daily", "compustat_annual", "compustat_quarterly", "ccm_links" (mirroring domain = "wrds"). Internally, every domain = "pseudo" call funnels simulate_pseudo_data(), unexported router dispatches per-dataset generators. Per-dataset entry points (download_data_pseudo_crsp(), download_data_pseudo_compustat(), download_data_pseudo_ccm_links()) remain exported direct use. generators accept n_assets seed arguments; identical (seed, n_assets) yields identifier universe across datasets, pseudo CRSP Compustat join cleanly via add_ccm_links = TRUE ccm_links. Daily CRSP generated weekdays . Added download_factor_library_grid() fetch tidy-finance/factor-library-grid dataset Hugging Face. Also accessible via download_data("tidyfinance", "factor_library_grid").

title: tidyfinance 0.6.0
text: Added test-coverage.yaml workflow badge README. Added tests get coverage 100% (excl. set_wrds_credentials()). Fama-French factor data now downloaded parsed internally via httr2, frenchdata longer declared Imports. behavior download_data_factors_ff() unchanged. download_data("tidyfinance", "factor_library", ...) now honors canonical start_date end_date arguments, filtering returned portfolio returns requested range. omitted, full history returned standard “Returning full data set” message emitted (via validate_dates()). Previously arguments accepted silently ignored factor library. Removed using-tidyfinance dates--tidyfinance vignettes. predated current download_data() interface superseded package manuscript. knitr rmarkdown longer declared Suggests, VignetteBuilder dropped DESCRIPTION. download_data("tidyfinance", "factor_library", ids = <vector>) now delegates directly download_factor_library_ids(), bypassing grid filter. Passing ids together filter arguments raises informative error. Renamed list_supported_types() list_supported_datasets() (#242). old name remains exported soft-deprecated alias forwards new function. Internal helpers renamed accordingly (e.g. list_supported_types_ff() -> list_supported_datasets_ff()). download_data_constituents() now drops symbols equal "-". Renamed only_us parameter download_data_wrds_compustat() only_usd reflect filter keeps USD-denominated shares . old name deprecated forwards only_usd warning. Removed arrow, glue, stringr dependencies added nanoparquet.

title: tidyfinance 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-12

title: tidyfinance 0.5.0
text: Added implement_portfolio_sort() convenience wrapper combines sample construction filtering portfolio return computation single call. Added download_data_risk_free() download process risk-free rate data FRED, splicing TB3MS (pre-2001) DTB4WK (2001 onwards) monthly data, using DTB3 daily data. Also accessible via download_data("tidyfinance", "risk_free"). Updated download_data_wrds_crsp() use download_data_risk_free() (FRED-based) instead Kenneth French risk-free rate computing excess returns. Added download_data_risk_free(). Added only_us parameter download_data_wrds_compustat(). Added new parameters common CRSP transformation tasks (add_ccm_links, adjust_volume) download_data_wrds_crsp(). Added prc_adj "crsp_monthly" version "v1". Added adjust_volume parameter "crsp_daily" version "v1" "v2" download_data_wrds_compustat(). Added compute_rolling_value(). Added output parameter estimate_model() also return t-stats residuals. Added join_lagged_values(). Added indexes list_supported_indexes(). Added download_data_huggingface() get_available_huggingface_files(). support type = "hf_high_frequency_sp500". Deprecated type parameter favor domain dataset. Added detail parameter estimate_fama_macbeth() include average n_obs, r_squared, adj_r_squared. Removed lower bound excess returns download_data_wrds_crsp(). Removed add_lag_columns() favor add_lagged_columns(). Added domain "tidyfinance" datasets "high_frequency_sp500", "factor_library", "risk_free".

title: tidyfinance 0.5.0
text: Removed renv due lack benefits. Moved optional dependencies imports improved user experience (except furrr).

title: tidyfinance 0.5.0
text: Removed erroneous time zone adjustment download_data_wrds_trace_enhanced() #133. Replaced tabs list_supported_types_ff() underscores #134. compute_portfolio_returns() implement_portfolio_sort() now apply min_portfolio_size reported portfolio cross-section. bivariate sorts firm count per (main_portfolio, date) summed across secondary buckets, per (main, secondary, date) cell . Previously, setting min_portfolio_size number cells (e.g. n_main * n_secondary) silently voided every cell. Univariate behaviour unchanged. default changed 0L 1L, reported portfolio required least one observation default; pass min_portfolio_size = 0L deactivate check. param documentation also corrected reflect small portfolios receive NA (zero). compute_long_short_returns() longer errors object 'top' found input panel contains one distinct portfolio (e.g., assign_portfolio() collapsed single bucket constant sorting variable). long-short return now NA dates, consistent “investment, return”, instead crashing.
